title: Supply storage holder and system
abstract: A storage system (10) includes at least one cabinet (12), a plurality of shelves (40) adjustably positioned within the at least one cabinet (12), and a plurality of scales (18) removably attached to at least one of the shelves (40), each of the scales (18) including a load sensor (130). The storage system (10) also includes a plurality of holders (14a, 14b), each of the holders (14a, 14b) being configured to hold at least one container (16a, 16b) containing medical supplies, and each of the holders (14a, 14b) being supported by a respective one of the scales (18). The storage system (10) also includes a receiver (20) in operative communication with each of the load sensors (130). Each of the load sensors (130) is configured to continuously measure a weight of the medical supplies contained in the respective at least one container (16a, 16b) held by the respective holder (14a, 14b), and to communicate each detected weight to the receiver (20).
claims: Claims 1. A storage system, comprising: at least one cabinet; a plurality of shelves adjustably positioned within the at least one cabinet; a plurality of scales removably attached to at least one of the shelves, each of the scales including a load sensor; a plurality of holders, each of the holders being configured to hold at least one container containing medical supplies, and each of the holders being supported by a respective one of the scales; and a receiver in operative communication with each of the load sensors, wherein each of the load sensors is configured to continuously measure a weight of the medical supplies contained in the respective at least one container held by the respective holder, and to communicate each detected weight to the receiver. 2. The storage system of claim 1 , wherein the receiver is configured to monitor an inventory condition of the medical supplies contained in each of the at least one container held by each holder in response to each detected weight. 3. The storage system of claim 2, wherein the receiver is configured to display the inventory condition to a monitoring station. 4. The storage system of claim 1 , wherein each of the holders includes a plurality of longitudinally extending resilient finger grips for frictionally engaging the respective at least one container. 5. The storage system of claim 1 , wherein each of the holders includes a storage slot configured to receive one or more of the medical supplies which have been removed from the respective at least one container. 6. The storage system of claim 1 , wherein each of the holders is longitudinally slidable relative to the respective scale. 7. The storage system of claim 6, further comprising: a plurality of support plates, each of the support plates being supported by a respective one of the scales, wherein each of the holders slidably receives the respective support plate. 8. The storage system of claim 7, wherein each of the holders includes a stop hole and each of the support plates includes a flexible tab configured to releasably engage the stop hole of the respective holder for limiting longitudinal sliding of the respective holder relative to the respective scale. 9. The storage system of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of holders includes a first plurality of holders each having a first width, and a second plurality of holders each having a second width less than the first width. 10. The storage system of claim 1 , wherein each of the scales includes a plurality of mounting feet and each of the shelves includes a plurality of mounting holes, each of the mounting holes being configured to receive a respective one of the mounting feet. 1 1. The storage system of claim 1 , wherein the at least one cabinet is mounted to a mobile cart. 12. The storage system of claim 1 , wherein the at least one cabinet includes a plurality of cabinets mounted on a rack. 13. The storage system of claim 1 , wherein the at least one cabinet includes a plurality of cabinets interconnected in a matrix and mounted on a stand. 14. A medical supply holder, comprising: a U-shaped frame having a bottom wall and first and second side walls spaced apart from each other to define an opening, wherein the bottom wall includes a longitudinally extending ridge defining a longitudinally extending bottom channel; and first and second longitudinally extending box supports positioned on interior sides of the first and second side walls, respectively, at an aligned height, wherein the first and second side walls and first and second box supports collectively define a main cavity which is sized and configured to receive at least one container containing medical supplies. 15. The medical supply holder of claim 14, wherein the bottom wall, first and second side walls, and first and second box supports collectively define a storage slot configured to receive one or more of the medical supplies which have been removed from the at least one container. 16. The medical supply holder of claim 14, further comprising: first and second longitudinally extending hook-shaped rails positioned on an exterior side of the bottom wall, such that the bottom wall and the first and second rails define first and second longitudinally extending grooves configured to slidably engage a support plate of a scale. 17. The medical supply holder of claim 14, wherein at least one stop hole is provided in the ridge to assist in retaining the holder on a scale. 18. The medical supply holder of claim 14, wherein the frame is constructed of a plastic material. 19. The medical supply holder of claim 14, further comprising: a plurality of longitudinally extending resilient finger grips positioned on interior sides of the first and second side walls for frictionally engaging the at least one container. 20. The medical supply holder of claim 19, wherein the finger grips are constructed of a plastic material having a hardness of approximately 40 Shore A.
